390	## Backup & Restore Procedure
391	
392	### Daily Automated Backup (pg_dump)
393	
394	Set up a cron job or use the scheduler container. Retains 30 days of snapshots.
395	
396	```bash
397	#!/bin/bash
398	BACKUP_DIR="/backups/researchhub"
399	TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
400	RETENTION_DAYS=30
401	
402	mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"
403	
404	# Create compressed backup
405	docker compose exec -T db pg_dump \
406	  -U researchhub \
407	  -d researchhub \
408	  --format=custom \
409	  --compress=9 \
410	  > "$BACKUP_DIR/researchhub_${TIMESTAMP}.dump"
411	
412	# Verify backup integrity
413	docker compose exec -T db pg_restore \
414	  --list "$BACKUP_DIR/researchhub_${TIMESTAMP}.dump" > /dev/null 2>&1
415	
416	if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
417	  echo "[$(date)] Backup successful: researchhub_${TIMESTAMP}.dump"
418	else
419	  echo "[$(date)] ERROR: Backup verification failed!" >&2
420	fi
421	
422	# Prune backups older than retention period
423	find "$BACKUP_DIR" -name "*.dump" -mtime +${RETENTION_DAYS} -delete
424	echo "[$(date)] Pruned backups older than ${RETENTION_DAYS} days"
425	```
426	
427	### Restore Procedure
428	
429	Tested monthly as per policy.
430	
431	```bash
432	# 1. Stop all application containers (keep DB running)
433	docker compose stop app queue scheduler nginx
434	
435	# 2. Identify the backup to restore
436	ls -la /backups/researchhub/
437	# Example: researchhub_20260408_000000.dump
438	
439	# 3. Drop and recreate the database
440	docker compose exec db psql -U researchhub -c \
441	  "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS researchhub_restore;"
442	docker compose exec db psql -U researchhub -c \
443	  "CREATE DATABASE researchhub_restore OWNER researchhub;"
444	docker compose exec db psql -U researchhub -d researchhub_restore -c \
445	  "CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;"
446	
447	# 4. Restore from backup
448	docker compose exec -T db pg_restore \
449	  -U researchhub \
450	  -d researchhub_restore \
451	  --no-owner \
452	  --no-privileges \
453	  < /backups/researchhub/researchhub_20260408_000000.dump
454	
455	# 5. Verify row counts
456	docker compose exec db psql -U researchhub -d researchhub_restore -c \
457	  "SELECT 'users' as tbl, count(*) FROM users
458	   UNION ALL SELECT 'services', count(*) FROM services
459	   UNION ALL SELECT 'reservations', count(*) FROM reservations
460	   UNION ALL SELECT 'audit_logs', count(*) FROM audit_logs;"
461	
462	# 6. Swap databases (atomic rename)
463	docker compose exec db psql -U researchhub -c "
464	  SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid)
465	    FROM pg_stat_activity
466	    WHERE datname = 'researchhub' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid();
467	  ALTER DATABASE researchhub RENAME TO researchhub_old;
468	  ALTER DATABASE researchhub_restore RENAME TO researchhub;"
469	
470	# 7. Restart all containers
471	docker compose start app queue scheduler nginx
472	
473	# 8. Verify application health
474	curl -s https://localhost:4443/up | head -1
475	
476	# 9. Drop the old database after confirming everything works
477	docker compose exec db psql -U researchhub -c \
478	  "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS researchhub_old;"
479	```
480	
481	### Monthly Restore Drill
482	
483	A restore drill checklist with dated records is maintained at [`docs/ops/restore-drill-checklist.md`](docs/ops/restore-drill-checklist.md). Each monthly drill must be logged with operator, backup file, result, and notes. See that document for the full procedure and compliance requirements.
484	
485	### Point-in-Time Recovery Notes
486	
487	- All state changes are recorded in the immutable `audit_logs` table (enforced at both application and DB level via PostgreSQL triggers that block UPDATE/DELETE)
488	- Reservation state transitions are audited and can be replayed if needed
489	- Import batches track all changes with error logs for traceability
490	
491	### Architecture Decision Records
492	
493	- [`docs/architecture-decision-api-livewire.md`](docs/architecture-decision-api-livewire.md) -- Documents the dual-path architecture (REST API + Livewire direct service access) and why Livewire components share Action/Service classes rather than routing through HTTP API endpoints.
494	
495	---
496	
497	## Testing
498	
499	### Run All Tests
500	
501	```bash
502	# Via Docker
503	docker compose exec app php artisan test
504	
505	# Local
506	php artisan test
507	
508	# With coverage
509	php artisan test --coverage
510	```
511	
512	### Test Suites
513	
514	| Suite | File | Count | Covers |
515	|---|---|---|---|
516	| Login | `tests/Feature/Auth/LoginTest.php` | 9 | Login success/failure, lockout, CAPTCHA, password expiry |
517	| Password Policy | `tests/Feature/Auth/PasswordPolicyTest.php` | 8 | Complexity rules, history prevention, pruning |
518	| Session/Logout | `tests/Feature/Auth/SessionAndLogoutTest.php` | 4 | Logout, audit log, expired password redirect |
519	| Step-Up Auth | `tests/Feature/Auth/StepUpAuthTest.php` | 4 | Verify success/failure, audit logging |
520	| CAPTCHA Unit | `tests/Unit/CaptchaServiceTest.php` | 4 | Generation, verification, case-insensitivity, single-use |
521	| Catalog | `tests/Feature/Catalog/CatalogTest.php` | 8 | Search, filters, favorites, RBAC, recently viewed |
522	| Reservations | `tests/Feature/Reservation/ReservationTest.php` | 13 | Full lifecycle, penalties, freeze, check-in windows |
523	| Import/Export | `tests/Feature/ImportExport/ImportExportTest.php` | 12 | CSV/JSON parsing, export exclusions, duplicate detection |
524	| Adversarial Boundary | `tests/Unit/ReservationBoundaryTest.php` | 10 | Time-window spoofing, breach edge cases |
525	| CAPTCHA Adversarial | `tests/Unit/CaptchaAdversarialTest.php` | 5 | Payload tampering, replay, format validation |
526	
527	---
528	
529	## Security Model
530	
531	### Authentication
532	
533	- Strictly offline -- no external OAuth, SAML, or reCAPTCHA
534	- Sessions stored in PostgreSQL (`SESSION_DRIVER=database`)
535	- Session idle timeout: 20 minutes (with 2-minute JavaScript warning modal)
536	- Brute-force: 5 failures trigger 15-minute lockout; CAPTCHA required after 3 failures
537	- Step-up auth: 5-minute elevated token for critical actions
538	
539	### Authorization (RBAC)
540	
541	| Action | Learner | Editor | Admin |
542	|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
543	| Browse catalog | Yes | Yes | Yes |
544	| Book reservations | Yes | -- | -- |
545	| Create/edit services | -- | Yes | Yes |
546	| Manage time slots | -- | Yes | Yes |
547	| Import data | -- | -- | Yes |
548	| Export data (step-up required) | -- | -- | Yes |
549	| Manage policies | -- | -- | Yes |
550	
551	### Data Protection
552	
553	- Sensitive fields (`phone`, `external_id`) encrypted at rest via Laravel `encrypted` cast (AES-256-CBC)
554	- Passwords hashed with bcrypt (12 rounds)
555	- Passwords NEVER exported
556	- Audit logs are append-only (no UPDATE/DELETE in application layer)
557	- TLS recommended for local network via Nginx configuration
558	
559	### Reservation Policy Enforcement
560	
561	| Rule | Value |
562	|---|---|
563	| Pending expiry | 30 minutes |
564	| Free cancellation window | > 24 hours before start |
565	| Late cancellation penalty | $25.00 fee or 50 points |
566	| No-show breach threshold | 2 within 60 days |
567	| Booking freeze duration | 7 days |
568	| Check-in window | -15 min to +10 min from start |
569	| Late arrival classification | Partial attendance (cannot extend) |
570	
571	---
572	
573	## Project Structure
574	
575	```
576	repo/
577	|-- app/
578	|   |-- Actions/           # Business logic (Auth, Catalog)
579	|   |-- Console/Commands/  # Artisan commands (ProcessNoShows)
580	|   |-- Enums/             # UserRole, ReservationStatus, AuditAction
581	|   |-- Http/Middleware/    # CheckRole, StepUpAuth, SessionTimeout, EnsurePasswordNotExpired
582	|   |-- Jobs/              # ExpireUnconfirmedReservation, ProcessImportChunk
583	|   |-- Livewire/          # UI components (Auth, Catalog, Reservations, Admin)
584	|   |-- Models/            # 14 Eloquent models
585	|   |-- Policies/          # ReservationPolicy, ServicePolicy
586	|   |-- Providers/         # AppServiceProvider (policy registration)
587	|   +-- Services/          # Domain services (6 total)
588	|-- bootstrap/app.php      # Middleware registration
589	|-- database/
590	|   |-- migrations/        # 9 migration files
591	|   +-- seeders/           # DatabaseSeeder with sample data
592	|-- docker/
593	|   |-- nginx/default.conf
594	|   |-- php/Dockerfile, start.sh, php-local.ini
595	|   +-- postgres/init.sql  # pg_trgm + unaccent extensions
596	|-- docker-compose.yml     # 6 containers
597	|-- resources/views/       # 13 Blade templates
598	|-- routes/
599	|   |-- web.php            # All routes with middleware
600	|   +-- console.php        # Scheduler registration
601	+-- tests/                 # 10 test files, 70+ test cases
602	```
603	
604	---
605	
606	## Operational Compliance
607	
608	### Audit Trail Immutability
609	
610	All state changes are recorded in the `audit_logs` table which is protected at two levels:
611	- **Application level:** Eloquent model guards (`AuditLog::booted()`) throw `RuntimeException` on update/delete attempts
612	- **Database level:** PostgreSQL triggers (`trg_audit_log_no_update`, `trg_audit_log_no_delete`) block UPDATE/DELETE operations at the DB engine level (see migration `0004_01_01_000002_enforce_audit_log_immutability.php`)
613	
614	### Monthly Restore Drills
615	
616	Backup restore verification drills are conducted monthly per disaster recovery policy. Drill records with dated results, operator, and notes are maintained at [`docs/ops/restore-drill-checklist.md`](docs/ops/restore-drill-checklist.md).
617	
618	### Penalty Policy Configuration
619	
620	Late cancellation and no-show penalty parameters (mode, fee amount, points value) are admin-configurable via the Data Dictionary system under type `penalty_config`. Changes are audit-logged and take effect immediately via cache invalidation.

## Implementation Plan: API Auth Security Parity

### Problem Summary

The API login endpoint (`POST /api/auth/login`) in `AuthController::login()` bypasses all security controls that the web path enforces through the `AttemptLogin` action. Specifically missing: brute-force protection, account lockout, CAPTCHA requirement signaling, disabled account checks, device fingerprint anomaly detection, and password expiry handling. Additionally, the `EnsurePasswordNotExpired` middleware is only applied to web routes, not API routes.

### Architecture Observation

The project already follows a dual-path pattern: Livewire components and API controllers share logic through Action classes (e.g., `PerformLogout` is used in `routes/web.php` line 27). The `LoginForm` Livewire component correctly delegates to `AttemptLogin`. The API controller does not follow this pattern -- it duplicates (incomplete) auth logic inline.

---

### Step 1: Refactor `AttemptLogin` to decouple session concerns

**File:** `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Actions/Auth/AttemptLogin.php`

The current `AttemptLogin::execute()` calls `Auth::login($user)` and `session()->regenerate()` at line 109-110. These are session-specific operations that do not apply to API/token auth. The action needs to be refactored so the caller controls authentication establishment.

**Changes:**
- Add an optional parameter `bool $establishSession = true` (or an enum `AuthMode::Session | AuthMode::Token`).
- When `$establishSession` is `false`, skip `Auth::login($user)` and `session()->regenerate()` on lines 109-110. Instead, just return the successful result with the `$user` attached.
- Add a `public readonly ?User $user` property to `LoginResult` so the caller can access the authenticated user after a successful credential check.

**Specific changes to `LoginResult`:**

**File:** `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Actions/Auth/LoginResult.php`

- Add `public readonly ?User $user = null` to the constructor.
- Update `success()` and `passwordExpired()` static factories to accept an optional `?User $user` parameter and pass it through.
- This allows the API controller to retrieve the user for token creation without re-querying.

**Changes to `AttemptLogin::execute()`:**

- Add parameter: `bool $establishSession = true`
- At line 109, wrap `Auth::login($user)` and `session()->regenerate()` in `if ($establishSession)`.
- At lines 117 and 119, pass `$user` into `LoginResult::passwordExpired($user)` and `LoginResult::success($user)`.

This is backward-compatible: the Livewire `LoginForm` continues calling without the extra parameter and gets session-based auth as before.

---

### Step 2: Rewrite `AuthController::login()` to use `AttemptLogin`

**File:** `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php`

Replace the current `login()` method (lines 16-39) entirely. The new implementation:

```php
public function login(Request $request, AttemptLogin $action): JsonResponse
{
    $request->validate([
        'username' => 'required|string',
        'password' => 'required|string',
        'captcha'  => 'nullable|string',
    ]);

    $result = $action->execute(
        $request->username,
        $request->password,
        $request->captcha,
        establishSession: false,
    );

    if ($result->isSuccess()) {
        $token = $result->user->createToken('api')->plainTextToken;
        return response()->json([
            'user'  => new UserResource($result->user),
            'token' => $token,
        ]);
    }

    if ($result->isPasswordExpired()) {
        return response()->json([
            'error'   => 'password_expired',
            'message' => $result->message,
        ], 403);
    }

    if ($result->isCaptchaRequired()) {
        return response()->json([
            'error'   => 'captcha_required',
            'message' => $result->message,
        ], 422);
    }

    // locked or failed
    $statusCode = $result->status === 'locked' ? 423 : 401;
    return response()->json([
        'error'   => $result->status,
        'message' => $result->message,
    ], $statusCode);
}
```

Key decisions:
- Uses Sanctum token (`createToken`) instead of session auth, since this is the API path. The current code oddly uses `session()->regenerate()` for an API endpoint -- this is incorrect for a stateless API consumer.
- Maps each `LoginResult` status to an appropriate HTTP status code and structured JSON error body so API consumers can programmatically handle each case.
- Remove the unused `Hash` import from the file (it was only needed for the old inline password check; `stepUpVerify` still uses it so keep it if needed -- actually checking line 65, `Hash::check` is still used in `stepUpVerify`, so keep the import).

Also remove the `AuditService` parameter from `login()` since auditing is now handled inside `AttemptLogin`.

---

### Step 3: Apply `EnsurePasswordNotExpired` middleware to API routes

**File:** `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/routes/api.php`

The middleware already handles JSON responses correctly (see `EnsurePasswordNotExpired` line 17: `$request->expectsJson()`), so no changes to the middleware itself are needed.

**Change:** Add `password.not-expired` to the authenticated API route group. Update line 13 from:

```php
Route::middleware('auth')->group(function () {
```

to:

```php
Route::middleware(['auth', 'password.not-expired'])->group(function () {
```

This is safe because `EnsurePasswordNotExpired` already returns a 403 JSON response for API requests. The password change endpoint is not inside the API routes (it is a web-only Livewire route), which is correct -- an API consumer with an expired password gets a clear `password_expired` error and must change their password through the web UI or a dedicated API endpoint (see Step 5 below for optional consideration).

---

### Step 4: Verify role and step-up middleware consistency

Looking at the current state:
- **Web routes** (lines 55-59, 65-70): `role:editor,admin` for service management, `role:admin` + `step-up` for admin panel.
- **API routes** (lines 27-30, 43-60): `role:editor,admin` for catalog management, `role:admin` + `step-up` for admin routes.

These are already consistent. The `CheckRole` middleware at `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Middleware/CheckRole.php` redirects to `login` route for unauthenticated users (line 18), which is a web-oriented response. For API requests, the `auth` middleware will already have returned a 401 before `CheckRole` runs, so this is acceptable. However, for defense-in-depth:

**File:** `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Middleware/CheckRole.php`

Change line 16-18 to return a JSON 401 for API requests:

```php
if ($user === null) {
    if ($request->expectsJson()) {
        return response()->json(['error' => 'Unauthenticated.'], 401);
    }
    return redirect()->route('login');
}
```

And change the `abort(403)` on line 20 to also provide a JSON response for API:

```php
if (!in_array($user->role->value, $roles)) {
    if ($request->expectsJson()) {
        return response()->json(['error' => 'Forbidden.'], 403);
    }
    abort(403, 'You do not have permission to access this resource.');
}
```

---

### Step 5: Update `AuthController::logout()` for token-based auth

**File:** `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php`

The current `logout()` method (lines 46-55) calls `Auth::guard('web')->logout()` and invalidates the session. Since Step 2 switches API auth to Sanctum tokens, the logout should revoke the token instead:

```php
public function logout(Request $request, AuditService $audit): JsonResponse
{
    $audit->log(AuditAction::Logout, $request->user()->id);
    $request->user()->currentAccessToken()->delete();
    return response()->json(['message' => 'Logged out.']);
}
```

Correspondingly, update the API auth middleware from `'auth'` to `'auth:sanctum'` in `routes/api.php` line 13:

```php
Route::middleware(['auth:sanctum', 'password.not-expired'])->group(function () {
```

---

### Step 6: Write tests

**New file:** `tests/Feature/Api/ApiAuthTest.php`

Test cases mirroring the existing `LoginTest.php` but hitting the API endpoint:

1. `test_api_login_success_returns_token` -- POST `/api/auth/login` with valid credentials returns 200 with `token` and `user` keys.
2. `test_api_login_fails_with_wrong_password` -- returns 401.
3. `test_api_login_fails_for_disabled_account` -- returns 401 with disabled message.
4. `test_api_login_locks_after_5_failed_attempts` -- 5 bad attempts, then returns 423.
5. `test_api_login_returns_captcha_required` -- user with 3+ failed attempts gets 422 with `captcha_required` error.
6. `test_api_login_returns_password_expired` -- user with stale `password_updated_at` gets 403 with `password_expired` error.
7. `test_api_protected_routes_require_valid_password` -- authenticated user with expired password gets 403 from `EnsurePasswordNotExpired` on a protected route.
8. `test_api_admin_routes_require_admin_role` -- non-admin user gets 403.
9. `test_api_admin_routes_require_step_up` -- admin without step-up gets 403 with `step_up_required`.
10. `test_api_logout_revokes_token` -- token no longer works after logout.

---

### Implementation Sequence

1. **`LoginResult.php`** -- Add `?User $user` property (non-breaking, additive change).
2. **`AttemptLogin.php`** -- Add `$establishSession` parameter, conditionally skip session ops, pass user to result (non-breaking, default preserves existing behavior).
3. **`AuthController.php`** -- Rewrite `login()` and `logout()` to use `AttemptLogin` and Sanctum tokens.
4. **`routes/api.php`** -- Switch to `auth:sanctum`, add `password.not-expired` middleware.
5. **`CheckRole.php`** -- Add JSON response handling for API requests.
6. **`tests/Feature/Api/ApiAuthTest.php`** -- Full coverage of all API auth scenarios.

### Potential Risks

- **Session-to-token migration**: If any existing API consumers rely on session-based auth (the current code uses `session()->regenerate()`), switching to Sanctum tokens is a breaking change. Check if any frontend JS uses the API with session cookies. If so, keep session auth as an option or do a phased rollout.
- **CAPTCHA in API context**: API consumers cannot render a visual CAPTCHA. The `captcha_required` response signals the client must solve a CAPTCHA challenge. Ensure the API can serve CAPTCHA images via a separate endpoint (e.g., `GET /api/auth/captcha`), or consider using an invisible/reCAPTCHA token approach for API consumers.
- **`StepUpAuth` middleware** uses `session()` which won't work for stateless Sanctum token auth. If API admin routes need step-up, the middleware needs a dual-mode check (session for web, cache/DB timestamp for token auth). This is already partially an issue since the middleware already returns JSON for `expectsJson()` -- but the `elevate()` and `isElevated()` methods store in session. For the API flow via `AuthController::stepUpVerify()`, since the request goes through the `auth` middleware and uses sessions, this may already work if Sanctum is configured in stateful mode. Verify your `config/sanctum.php` `stateful` domains.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Actions/Auth/AttemptLogin.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Actions/Auth/LoginResult.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/routes/api.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Middleware/CheckRole.php

```

---

### Step 5: Update `AuthController::logout()` for token-based auth

**File:** `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php`

The current `logout()` method (lines 46-55) calls `Auth::guard('web')->logout()` and invalidates the session. Since Step 2 switches API auth to Sanctum tokens, the logout should revoke the token instead:

```php
public function logout(Request $request, AuditService $audit): JsonResponse
{
    $audit->log(AuditAction::Logout, $request->user()->id);
    $request->user()->currentAccessToken()->delete();
    return response()->json(['message' => 'Logged out.']);
}
```

Correspondingly, update the API auth middleware from `'auth'` to `'auth:sanctum'` in `routes/api.php` line 13:

```php
Route::middleware(['auth:sanctum', 'password.not-expired'])->group(function () {
```

---

### Step 6: Write tests

**New file:** `tests/Feature/Api/ApiAuthTest.php`

Test cases mirroring the existing `LoginTest.php` but hitting the API endpoint:

1. `test_api_login_success_returns_token` -- POST `/api/auth/login` with valid credentials returns 200 with `token` and `user` keys.
2. `test_api_login_fails_with_wrong_password` -- returns 401.
3. `test_api_login_fails_for_disabled_account` -- returns 401 with disabled message.
4. `test_api_login_locks_after_5_failed_attempts` -- 5 bad attempts, then returns 423.
5. `test_api_login_returns_captcha_required` -- user with 3+ failed attempts gets 422 with `captcha_required` error.
6. `test_api_login_returns_password_expired` -- user with stale `password_updated_at` gets 403 with `password_expired` error.
7. `test_api_protected_routes_require_valid_password` -- authenticated user with expired password gets 403 from `EnsurePasswordNotExpired` on a protected route.
8. `test_api_admin_routes_require_admin_role` -- non-admin user gets 403.
9. `test_api_admin_routes_require_step_up` -- admin without step-up gets 403 with `step_up_required`.
10. `test_api_logout_revokes_token` -- token no longer works after logout.

---

### Implementation Sequence

1. **`LoginResult.php`** -- Add `?User $user` property (non-breaking, additive change).
2. **`AttemptLogin.php`** -- Add `$establishSession` parameter, conditionally skip session ops, pass user to result (non-breaking, default preserves existing behavior).
3. **`AuthController.php`** -- Rewrite `login()` and `logout()` to use `AttemptLogin` and Sanctum tokens.
4. **`routes/api.php`** -- Switch to `auth:sanctum`, add `password.not-expired` middleware.
5. **`CheckRole.php`** -- Add JSON response handling for API requests.
6. **`tests/Feature/Api/ApiAuthTest.php`** -- Full coverage of all API auth scenarios.

### Potential Risks

- **Session-to-token migration**: If any existing API consumers rely on session-based auth (the current code uses `session()->regenerate()`), switching to Sanctum tokens is a breaking change. Check if any frontend JS uses the API with session cookies. If so, keep session auth as an option or do a phased rollout.
- **CAPTCHA in API context**: API consumers cannot render a visual CAPTCHA. The `captcha_required` response signals the client must solve a CAPTCHA challenge. Ensure the API can serve CAPTCHA images via a separate endpoint (e.g., `GET /api/auth/captcha`), or consider using an invisible/reCAPTCHA token approach for API consumers.
- **`StepUpAuth` middleware** uses `session()` which won't work for stateless Sanctum token auth. If API admin routes need step-up, the middleware needs a dual-mode check (session for web, cache/DB timestamp for token auth). This is already partially an issue since the middleware already returns JSON for `expectsJson()` -- but the `elevate()` and `isElevated()` methods store in session. For the API flow via `AuthController::stepUpVerify()`, since the request goes through the `auth` middleware and uses sessions, this may already work if Sanctum is configured in stateful mode. Verify your `config/sanctum.php` `stateful` domains.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Actions/Auth/AttemptLogin.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Actions/Auth/LoginResult.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/routes/api.php
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Http/Middleware/CheckRole.php

I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Here is the detailed implementation plan for all 5 issues.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Issue 1: Ensure API and Livewire share identical validation/auth/audit logic

**Diagnosis**: Your instinct is correct. Forcing Livewire to make HTTP loopback calls to its own API is an anti-pattern in Laravel. The existing dual-path architecture through shared Actions/Services is sound. However, there are real gaps:

- **Validation is NOT actually shared.** The architecture doc claims "both paths use the same validation rules (from DataDictionaryService and Action classes)" but this is false. `CatalogController::store()` (line 42-53) has hardcoded rules, `ServiceManager::save()` (line 49-54) has different hardcoded rules, and neither consults `DataDictionaryService::getValidationRules()`. This overlaps with Issue 5.
- **Audit logging is inconsistent.** `AdminController::changeRole()` calls `AuditService::log()` explicitly, but there is no evidence the Livewire admin components (`UserManager`, `DictionaryManager`) do the same for equivalent operations.
- **The API endpoints need integration tests proving parity.**

**Plan**:

1. **Update `docs/architecture-decision-api-livewire.md`** to add a "Shared Service Contract" section that explicitly states:
   - Validation rules are centralized in Action classes and merge dynamic rules from `DataDictionaryService::getValidationRules()`.
   - Both paths go through the same Action/Service methods that embed audit logging.
   - No business logic (including validation) lives in controllers or Livewire components.

2. **Create a validation trait or helper** (see Issue 5 below) that both API controllers and Livewire components use, ensuring identical rule sets.

3. **Add a Feature test** `tests/Feature/Integration/ApiLivewireParityTest.php` that verifies key operations (create service, create reservation) produce identical database state and audit log entries regardless of whether they go through the API controller or the Livewire component.

**Files to modify**: `docs/architecture-decision-api-livewire.md`
**Files to create**: `tests/Feature/Integration/ApiLivewireParityTest.php`

---

### Issue 2: Import pipeline file selection bug

**Diagnosis**: Confirmed. In `ImportManager::upload()` (line 53), `$this->file->store('imports', 'local')` returns a path like `imports/abc123.csv`, and `$fullPath` is correctly computed. But that path is never saved to the `ImportBatch` record. In `startProcessing()` (lines 114-118), the code:
```php
$path = storage_path('app/imports/' . basename($batch->filename));
$storedFiles = glob(storage_path('app/imports/*'));
$latestFile = end($storedFiles);
```
...first tries to build a path from the original client filename (which will NOT match the randomized stored filename), then falls back to globbing for the latest file in the imports directory. If two users upload simultaneously, this grabs the wrong file.

**Plan**:

1. **New migration** `database/migrations/2026_04_09_000001_add_stored_path_to_import_batches.php`:
   - Add `$table->string('stored_path', 500)->nullable()->after('filename');`

2. **Update `app/Models/ImportBatch.php`**:
   - Add `'stored_path'` to the `$fillable` array (line 12).

3. **Update `app/Livewire/Admin/ImportManager.php`**:
   - In `upload()` (around line 79), add `'stored_path' => $path` to the `ImportBatch::create()` call.
   - In `startProcessing()` (lines 113-125), replace the entire glob-based file lookup with:
     ```php
     $fullPath = storage_path('app/' . $batch->stored_path);
     if (!file_exists($fullPath)) {
         $this->errorMessage = 'Import file not found.';
         return;
     }
     $rows = $service->parseFile($fullPath, $batch->format);
     ```

4. **Update `tests/Feature/ImportExport/ImportExportTest.php`** to verify that `stored_path` is persisted and used correctly.

**Files to create**: `database/migrations/2026_04_09_000001_add_stored_path_to_import_batches.php`
**Files to modify**: `app/Models/ImportBatch.php`, `app/Livewire/Admin/ImportManager.php`

---

### Issue 3: Missing project/patent duplicate keys

**Diagnosis**: Confirmed. The `services` table has no `project_number` or `patent_number` columns. `ImportExportService::detectServiceDuplicates()` only checks exact ID match and title trigram similarity. The export method `exportServices()` also does not include these fields. The `getDestinationFields()` method for services does not list them.

**Plan**:

1. **New migration** `database/migrations/2026_04_09_000002_add_project_patent_to_services.php`:
   ```php
   Schema::table('services', function (Blueprint $table) {
       $table->string('project_number', 100)->nullable()->after('category');
       $table->string('patent_number', 100)->nullable()->after('project_number');
       $table->unique('project_number');
       $table->unique('patent_number');
   });
   ```

2. **Update `app/Models/Service.php`**:
   - Add `'project_number'` and `'patent_number'` to the `$fillable` array.

3. **Update `app/Services/ImportExportService.php`**:
   - In `getDestinationFields()` for services (line 111), add `'project_number'` and `'patent_number'` to the array.
   - In `detectServiceDuplicates()`, add exact-match checks for `project_number` and `patent_number` before the trigram similarity check. Pattern: if the incoming row has a mapped `project_number`, query `Service::where('project_number', $value)->first()`, and if found, push a conflict with `match_type => 'project_number'`. Same for `patent_number`.
   - In `exportServices()`, add `'project_number'` and `'patent_number'` to the export map (around line 38).
   - In `createServiceFromImport()`, include `'project_number'` and `'patent_number'` from `$data`.

4. **Update `app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php`**:
   - In `create()`, pass through `'project_number'` and `'patent_number'` from `$data`.
   - In `update()`, these are already handled by the `array_merge($data, ...)` pattern but confirm no filtering strips them.

5. **Update `app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`**:
   - Add public properties `$projectNumber` and `$patentNumber`.
   - Include them in `mount()` population and `save()` data array.

6. **Update `app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`**:
   - In `store()` validation, add `'project_number' => 'nullable|string|max:100|unique:services,project_number'` and `'patent_number' => 'nullable|string|max:100|unique:services,patent_number'`.
   - In `update()` validation, add similar rules with `unique:services,project_number,{id}`.

7. **Update `app/Http/Resources/ServiceResource.php`** to include the new fields.

8. **Update `app/Models/ImportConflict.php`** match_type comment or validation to recognize `'project_number'` and `'patent_number'` as valid match types.

9. **Update the import manager Blade view** `resources/views/livewire/catalog/service-manager.blade.php` to add input fields for both numbers.

**Files to create**: `database/migrations/2026_04_09_000002_add_project_patent_to_services.php`
**Files to modify**: `app/Models/Service.php`, `app/Services/ImportExportService.php`, `app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php`, `app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`, `app/Http/Resources/ServiceResource.php`, `resources/views/livewire/catalog/service-manager.blade.php`

---

### Issue 4: Tailwind CDN to local assets

**Diagnosis**: Confirmed. Both layout files use `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>`. However, the project already has everything needed for local Tailwind:
- `package.json` has `tailwindcss@^3.4.13`, `postcss@^8.4.47`, `autoprefixer@^10.4.20` as devDependencies.
- `tailwind.config.js` is properly configured with content paths.
- `postcss.config.js` is configured.
- `resources/css/app.css` has the three `@tailwind` directives.
- `vite.config.js` references `resources/css/app.css` and `resources/js/app.js`.

The only thing missing is replacing the CDN script tag with `@vite`.

**Plan**:

1. **Run `npm install`** to install node_modules (they may or may not exist already).

2. **Update `resources/views/components/layouts/app.blade.php`**:
   - Line 8: Replace `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>` with `@vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])`.

3. **Update `resources/views/components/layouts/guest.blade.php`**:
   - Line 8: Replace `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>` with `@vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])`.

4. **Verify `npm run build` completes** successfully to confirm Tailwind CSS is compiled.

**Files to modify**: `resources/views/components/layouts/app.blade.php`, `resources/views/components/layouts/guest.blade.php`

---

### Issue 5: Form rules not enforced on write paths

**Diagnosis**: Confirmed. `DataDictionaryService::getValidationRules('service')` builds Laravel validation arrays from the `form_rules` table, but no write path calls it. All three write paths use hardcoded rules:

- `ServiceManager::save()` (Livewire) - hardcoded at line 49-54
- `CatalogController::store()` (API) - hardcoded at line 42-53
- `CatalogController::update()` (API) - hardcoded at line 62-64
- `ReservationController::store()` (API) - hardcoded at line 33-34

The `ManageService` action class already injects `DataDictionaryService` (line 12) but never calls `getValidationRules()`.

**Plan**: Centralize validation into the Action classes, since they are the shared layer. Controllers and Livewire components should delegate to them.

1. **Create `app/Actions/Concerns/MergesDynamicRules.php`** trait:
   ```php
   trait MergesDynamicRules
   {
       protected function mergeRules(array $baseRules, string $entity, DataDictionaryService $dictService): array
       {
           $dynamicRules = $dictService->getValidationRules($entity);
           foreach ($dynamicRules as $field => $fieldRules) {
               if (isset($baseRules[$field])) {
                   // Merge: dynamic rules supplement base rules
                   $baseRules[$field] = array_unique(array_merge(
                       (array) $baseRules[$field],
                       $fieldRules,
                   ));
               } else {
                   $baseRules[$field] = $fieldRules;
               }
           }
           return $baseRules;
       }
   }
   ```

2. **Update `app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php`**:
   - Use the `MergesDynamicRules` trait.
   - Add a `validationRules(string $mode = 'create'): array` method that returns the base hardcoded rules merged with `$this->dictService->getValidationRules('service')`. The `$mode` parameter controls whether fields are `required` vs `sometimes|required` (for update).
   - Add a `validate(array $data, string $mode = 'create'): array` method that runs `Validator::make($data, $this->validationRules($mode))->validate()`.
   - Call `$this->validate($data)` at the top of `create()`.
   - Call `$this->validate($data, 'update')` at the top of `update()`.

3. **Update `app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`**:
   - Remove the hardcoded `$this->validate([...])` call in `save()`.
   - Instead, let the `ManageService` action's `validate()` throw a `ValidationException` which Livewire will catch and display automatically.
   - Or, call `$action->validationRules()` to get rules and pass them to Livewire's `$this->validate()` for the wire:model error-bag integration.
   - The second approach is better for UX because it populates Livewire's `$errors` bag. So: inject `ManageService` in `save()`, call `$this->validate($action->validationRules($this->isEditing ? 'update' : 'create'))`.

4. **Update `app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`**:
   - In `store()`, replace the hardcoded `$request->validate([...])` with `$request->validate($action->validationRules('create'))`.
   - In `update()`, replace with `$request->validate($action->validationRules('update'))`.

5. **For reservations**: Create `app/Actions/Reservation/ValidateReservation.php` or add a `validationRules()` method to `ReservationService` that merges dynamic rules for the `'reservation'` entity. Update `ReservationController::store()` accordingly.

6. **Add tests** `tests/Feature/Catalog/DynamicValidationTest.php` that:
   - Seed a `FormRule` for entity `'service'`, field `'title'`, rules `{min: 10}`.
   - Verify that both the API endpoint and the Livewire component reject a title shorter than 10 characters.

**Files to create**: `app/Actions/Concerns/MergesDynamicRules.php`, `tests/Feature/Catalog/DynamicValidationTest.php`
**Files to modify**: `app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php`, `app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/Api/ReservationController.php`

---

## Sequencing and Dependencies

| Order | Issue | Depends On | Risk |
|-------|-------|-----------|------|
| 1 | Issue 4 (Tailwind CDN) | None | Low - purely cosmetic, no logic changes |
| 2 | Issue 2 (Import file path) | None | Low - isolated migration + two file edits |
| 3 | Issue 3 (Project/patent numbers) | None | Medium - schema change touches multiple layers |
| 4 | Issue 5 (Dynamic validation) | None, but simplifies Issue 1 | Medium - refactors validation across all write paths |
| 5 | Issue 1 (API/Livewire parity) | Issue 5 | Low after Issue 5 - mostly documentation + tests |

Issues 2, 3, and 4 are independent and can be done in parallel. Issue 5 should come before Issue 1 because centralizing validation is the main concrete change that makes the "shared service" claim in the architecture doc actually true.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php` - Central action that must own validation rules for service create/update (Issue 1 + 5)
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Livewire/Admin/ImportManager.php` - Contains the file selection bug with glob fallback at lines 113-125 (Issue 2)
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Services/ImportExportService.php` - Duplicate detection must add project/patent matching, export must include new fields (Issue 3)
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Services/DataDictionaryService.php` - Already has `getValidationRules()` but nothing calls it; central to Issue 5
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/resources/views/components/layouts/app.blade.php` - CDN script tag on line 8 must become `@vite` directive (Issue 4)

**Diagnosis**: Confirmed. Both layout files use `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>`. However, the project already has everything needed for local Tailwind:

- `vite.config.js` references `resources/css/app.css` and `resources/js/app.js`.

The only thing missing is replacing the CDN script tag with `@vite`.

**Plan**:

1. **Run `npm install`** to install node_modules (they may or may not exist already).

2. **Update `resources/views/components/layouts/app.blade.php`**:
   - Line 8: Replace `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>` with `@vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])`.

3. **Update `resources/views/components/layouts/guest.blade.php`**:

### Issue 5: Form rules not enforced on write paths

**Diagnosis**: Confirmed. `DataDictionaryService::getValidationRules('service')` builds Laravel validation arrays from the `form_rules` table, but no write path calls it. All three write paths use hardcoded rules:

- `ServiceManager::save()` (Livewire) - hardcoded at line 49-54
- `CatalogController::store()` (API) - hardcoded at line 42-53
- `CatalogController::update()` (API) - hardcoded at line 62-64
- `ReservationController::store()` (API) - hardcoded at line 33-34

The `ManageService` action class already injects `DataDictionaryService` (line 12) but never calls `getValidationRules()`.

**Plan**: Centralize validation into the Action classes, since they are the shared layer. Controllers and Livewire components should delegate to them.

1. **Create `app/Actions/Concerns/MergesDynamicRules.php`** trait:
   ```php
   trait MergesDynamicRules
   {
       protected function mergeRules(array $baseRules, string $entity, DataDictionaryService $dictService): array
       {
           $dynamicRules = $dictService->getValidationRules($entity);
           foreach ($dynamicRules as $field => $fieldRules) {
               if (isset($baseRules[$field])) {
                   // Merge: dynamic rules supplement base rules
                   $baseRules[$field] = array_unique(array_merge(
                       (array) $baseRules[$field],
                       $fieldRules,
                   ));
               } else {
                   $baseRules[$field] = $fieldRules;
               }
           }
           return $baseRules;
       }
   }
   ```

2. **Update `app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php`**:
   - Use the `MergesDynamicRules` trait.
   - Add a `validationRules(string $mode = 'create'): array` method that returns the base hardcoded rules merged with `$this->dictService->getValidationRules('service')`. The `$mode` parameter controls whether fields are `required` vs `sometimes|required` (for update).
   - Add a `validate(array $data, string $mode = 'create'): array` method that runs `Validator::make($data, $this->validationRules($mode))->validate()`.
   - Call `$this->validate($data)` at the top of `create()`.
   - Call `$this->validate($data, 'update')` at the top of `update()`.

3. **Update `app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`**:
   - Remove the hardcoded `$this->validate([...])` call in `save()`.
   - Instead, let the `ManageService` action's `validate()` throw a `ValidationException` which Livewire will catch and display automatically.
   - Or, call `$action->validationRules()` to get rules and pass them to Livewire's `$this->validate()` for the wire:model error-bag integration.
   - The second approach is better for UX because it populates Livewire's `$errors` bag. So: inject `ManageService` in `save()`, call `$this->validate($action->validationRules($this->isEditing ? 'update' : 'create'))`.

4. **Update `app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`**:
   - In `store()`, replace the hardcoded `$request->validate([...])` with `$request->validate($action->validationRules('create'))`.
   - In `update()`, replace with `$request->validate($action->validationRules('update'))`.

5. **For reservations**: Create `app/Actions/Reservation/ValidateReservation.php` or add a `validationRules()` method to `ReservationService` that merges dynamic rules for the `'reservation'` entity. Update `ReservationController::store()` accordingly.

6. **Add tests** `tests/Feature/Catalog/DynamicValidationTest.php` that:
   - Seed a `FormRule` for entity `'service'`, field `'title'`, rules `{min: 10}`.
   - Verify that both the API endpoint and the Livewire component reject a title shorter than 10 characters.

**Files to create**: `app/Actions/Concerns/MergesDynamicRules.php`, `tests/Feature/Catalog/DynamicValidationTest.php`
**Files to modify**: `app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php`, `app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/Api/ReservationController.php`

|-------|-------|-----------|------|

Issues 2, 3, and 4 are independent and can be done in parallel. Issue 5 should come before Issue 1 because centralizing validation is the main concrete change that makes the "shared service" claim in the architecture doc actually true.

The web admin routes (`/admin/import`, `/admin/export`, `/admin/users`, `/admin/dictionaries`) all have `step-up` middleware. The test at line 122-128 says "Import route does NOT require step-up" but the web.php clearly shows `/admin/import` IS inside the `step-up` middleware group. Let me re-read that test.

Looking at the StepUpAuthIntegrationTest line 122-128:

This test asserts 200 but `/admin/import` is inside the `['role:admin', 'step-up']` middleware group in `routes/web.php` (line 65). Without step-up elevation, this should redirect/403. This test is either wrong or passing accidentally.

Here is the detailed implementation plan covering all four issues.

## Issue 1: Incomplete Audit Logging for Reservation Transitions

### Analysis

The `AuditAction` enum already has `ReservationCheckedOut` and `ReservationRescheduled` values defined but they are unused in the service. Three enum values are missing entirely: `ReservationExpired`, `ReservationNoShow`, and `ReservationPartialAttendance`.

Five methods in `ReservationService` have audit gaps:

1. **`reschedule()` (line 149)** -- Calls `createReservation()` internally (which logs `ReservationCreated`), and also updates the old reservation to Cancelled, but never logs a `ReservationRescheduled` action. The reschedule event itself is untracked.

2. **`checkIn()` (line 169)** -- Logs `ReservationCheckedIn` for both on-time and late arrivals. The `late` flag is in metadata, but there is no distinct audit for `PartialAttendance`. Whether this needs a separate action or just the metadata flag is a design choice; the requirement says to add a `ReservationPartialAttendance` action.

3. **`checkOut()` (line 192)** -- No audit at all. The enum has `ReservationCheckedOut` already defined but never used.

4. **`expireIfPending()` (line 214)** -- No audit. Needs new `ReservationExpired` enum value.

5. **`processNoShows()` (line 233)** -- No audit per-reservation. Needs new `ReservationNoShow` enum value.

### File Changes

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Enums/AuditAction.php`**

Add three new enum cases after the existing reservation cases (after line 31):

```php
case ReservationExpired = 'reservation_expired';
case ReservationNoShow = 'reservation_no_show';
case ReservationPartialAttendance = 'reservation_partial_attendance';
```

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Services/ReservationService.php`**

Five changes:

(a) In `reschedule()`, after line 162 (`$reservation->timeSlot->decrement('booked_count')`), add an audit log call before `createReservation`:

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationRescheduled, $reservation->user_id, [
    'old_reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'old_time_slot_id' => $reservation->time_slot_id,
    'new_time_slot_id' => $newTimeSlotId,
]);
```

(b) In `checkIn()`, split the audit logging. Replace the single `ReservationCheckedIn` call at line 184-187 with conditional logic:

```php
$auditAction = $isLate
    ? AuditAction::ReservationPartialAttendance
    : AuditAction::ReservationCheckedIn;

$this->audit->log($auditAction, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'late' => $isLate,
]);
```

(c) In `checkOut()`, add audit logging before the return at line 211:

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationCheckedOut, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'partial_attendance' => $reservation->getOriginal('status') === ReservationStatus::PartialAttendance->value,
]);
```

Note: Use `getOriginal('status')` because `update()` was just called. Alternatively, capture the status before update into a local variable.

(d) In `expireIfPending()`, add audit logging inside the transaction after line 229 (`$reservation->timeSlot->decrement('booked_count')`):

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationExpired, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'expired_after_minutes' => $this->penaltyConfig->getExpiryMinutes(),
]);
```

(e) In `processNoShows()`, add audit logging inside the per-reservation transaction (after the `Penalty::create` call around line 256):

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationNoShow, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'time_slot_id' => $reservation->time_slot_id,
], 'warning');

Use `'warning'` severity since no-shows trigger penalties.

## Issue 3: Step-Up Auth Test Contradiction

### Analysis

In `tests/Feature/Integration/StepUpAuthIntegrationTest.php`, line 122-128:

```php
public function test_import_does_not_require_step_up(): void
{
    $this->actingAs($this->admin);
    $response = $this->get('/admin/import');
    $response->assertStatus(200);
}
```

Looking at `routes/web.php` line 65:
```php
Route::middleware(['role:admin', 'step-up'])->prefix('admin')->group(function () {
    Route::get('/import', ImportManager::class)->name('admin.import');
    Route::get('/export', ExportManager::class)->name('admin.export');
    ...
});
```

`/admin/import` IS behind the `step-up` middleware. This test is incorrect -- it asserts 200 but should expect a redirect to `route('auth.step-up')`. This test may currently pass if a previous test in the class set `step_up_verified_at` in the session and session state leaked (though with `RefreshDatabase` and separate test instances this is unlikely). More likely it is a failing or skipped test.

### Fix

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/tests/Feature/Integration/StepUpAuthIntegrationTest.php`**

Change `test_import_does_not_require_step_up` to reflect reality. Two options:

**Option A** (if the intent was to confirm import DOES require step-up, matching export):
```php
public function test_import_requires_step_up(): void
{
    $this->actingAs($this->admin);
    $response = $this->get('/admin/import');
    $response->assertRedirect(route('auth.step-up'));
}
```

**Option B** (if the design intent was that import should NOT require step-up):
Move the import route out of the `step-up` middleware group in `routes/web.php`. This seems unlikely given the README states all admin routes require step-up.

Recommendation: Option A -- fix the test assertion. The route definition is correct; the test comment and assertion are wrong.

3. Run existing `ReservationTest` to verify nothing breaks

**Phase 2 -- Step-up test fix (one-line change)**
1. Fix `StepUpAuthIntegrationTest.php` line 122-128

**Phase 3 -- API test coverage (additive, no production code changes)**
1. Create `tests/Feature/Api/ApiAuthTest.php`
2. Create `tests/Feature/Api/ApiCatalogTest.php`
3. Create `tests/Feature/Api/ApiReservationTest.php`
4. Create `tests/Feature/Api/ApiAdminTest.php`
5. Create `tests/Feature/Api/ApiTenantIsolationTest.php`
6. Run full test suite

**Phase 4 -- Documentation/config cleanup**
1. Fix README container count
2. Update `.env.example` to match project config

---

## Potential Challenges

1. **`checkOut()` audit -- capturing pre-update status**: The `update()` call changes the status before the audit line runs. Either capture the old status into a variable before update, or use `$reservation->getOriginal('status')`.

2. **`expireIfPending()` runs inside a queue job**: The `AuditService` captures `request()->ip()` and `request()->userAgent()`. In a queue context, there is no HTTP request. The existing `$request ??= request()` fallback will return a synthetic request with null IP/user-agent. This is acceptable for system-triggered actions, but the metadata should include an indicator like `'triggered_by' => 'system'`.

3. **`processNoShows()` is a batch operation**: It loops through reservations. Each audit call will hit the database. For large batches, consider wrapping in a single transaction or accepting the per-record overhead (since no-show processing runs nightly via cron, performance is not critical).

4. **API tests and session-based auth**: The API routes use session-based auth (`middleware('auth')`), not Sanctum/token auth. Tests will use `$this->actingAs($user)` and send requests with `Accept: application/json` header (via `$this->getJson()`, `$this->postJson()`) so the middleware returns JSON 401/403 instead of HTML redirects.

5. **Step-up in API admin tests**: The `step-up` middleware checks `session('step_up_verified_at')`. In tests, you need to set this in the session before making the request. Use `$this->withSession(['step_up_verified_at' => now()])` on the test request chain.

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Services/ReservationService.php`
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Enums/AuditAction.php`
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/tests/Feature/Integration/StepUpAuthIntegrationTest.php`
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/routes/api.php`
- `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/.env.example`

Good, `.env` is not tracked. Now let me check the web routes more carefully to identify the step-up test contradiction. The web admin routes (`/admin/import`, `/admin/export`, `/admin/users`, `/admin/dictionaries`) all have `step-up` middleware. The test at line 122-128 says "Import route does NOT require step-up" but the web.php clearly shows `/admin/import` IS inside the `step-up` middleware group. Let me re-read that test.

Looking at the StepUpAuthIntegrationTest line 122-128:

```php
public function test_import_does_not_require_step_up(): void
{
    $this->actingAs($this->admin);
    $response = $this->get('/admin/import');
    $response->assertStatus(200);
}
```

This test asserts 200 but `/admin/import` is inside the `['role:admin', 'step-up']` middleware group in `routes/web.php` (line 65). Without step-up elevation, this should redirect/403. This test is either wrong or passing accidentally.

Now I have enough information to produce the full plan.

---

Here is the detailed implementation plan covering all four issues.

## Issue 1: Incomplete Audit Logging for Reservation Transitions

### Analysis

The `AuditAction` enum already has `ReservationCheckedOut` and `ReservationRescheduled` values defined but they are unused in the service. Three enum values are missing entirely: `ReservationExpired`, `ReservationNoShow`, and `ReservationPartialAttendance`.

Five methods in `ReservationService` have audit gaps:

1. **`reschedule()` (line 149)** -- Calls `createReservation()` internally (which logs `ReservationCreated`), and also updates the old reservation to Cancelled, but never logs a `ReservationRescheduled` action. The reschedule event itself is untracked.

2. **`checkIn()` (line 169)** -- Logs `ReservationCheckedIn` for both on-time and late arrivals. The `late` flag is in metadata, but there is no distinct audit for `PartialAttendance`. Whether this needs a separate action or just the metadata flag is a design choice; the requirement says to add a `ReservationPartialAttendance` action.

3. **`checkOut()` (line 192)** -- No audit at all. The enum has `ReservationCheckedOut` already defined but never used.

4. **`expireIfPending()` (line 214)** -- No audit. Needs new `ReservationExpired` enum value.

5. **`processNoShows()` (line 233)** -- No audit per-reservation. Needs new `ReservationNoShow` enum value.

### File Changes

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Enums/AuditAction.php`**

Add three new enum cases after the existing reservation cases (after line 31):

```php
case ReservationExpired = 'reservation_expired';
case ReservationNoShow = 'reservation_no_show';
case ReservationPartialAttendance = 'reservation_partial_attendance';
```

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/app/Services/ReservationService.php`**

Five changes:

(a) In `reschedule()`, after line 162 (`$reservation->timeSlot->decrement('booked_count')`), add an audit log call before `createReservation`:

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationRescheduled, $reservation->user_id, [
    'old_reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'old_time_slot_id' => $reservation->time_slot_id,
    'new_time_slot_id' => $newTimeSlotId,
]);
```

(b) In `checkIn()`, split the audit logging. Replace the single `ReservationCheckedIn` call at line 184-187 with conditional logic:

```php
$auditAction = $isLate
    ? AuditAction::ReservationPartialAttendance
    : AuditAction::ReservationCheckedIn;

$this->audit->log($auditAction, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'late' => $isLate,
]);
```

(c) In `checkOut()`, add audit logging before the return at line 211:

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationCheckedOut, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'partial_attendance' => $reservation->getOriginal('status') === ReservationStatus::PartialAttendance->value,
]);
```

Note: Use `getOriginal('status')` because `update()` was just called. Alternatively, capture the status before update into a local variable.

(d) In `expireIfPending()`, add audit logging inside the transaction after line 229 (`$reservation->timeSlot->decrement('booked_count')`):

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationExpired, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'expired_after_minutes' => $this->penaltyConfig->getExpiryMinutes(),
]);
```

(e) In `processNoShows()`, add audit logging inside the per-reservation transaction (after the `Penalty::create` call around line 256):

```php
$this->audit->log(AuditAction::ReservationNoShow, $reservation->user_id, [
    'reservation_id' => $reservation->id,
    'time_slot_id' => $reservation->time_slot_id,
], 'warning');
```

Use `'warning'` severity since no-shows trigger penalties.

---

## Issue 2: API Test Coverage

### Approach

Create four new test files following the existing patterns. The existing tests use `RefreshDatabase`, `User::create()` for setup, and standard Laravel HTTP testing (`$this->getJson()`, `$this->postJson()`, etc.). Since the API uses session-based auth (not tokens), tests will use `$this->actingAs($user)` then call JSON endpoints.

### File: `tests/Feature/Api/ApiAuthTest.php` (new)

Test cases to implement:

1. `test_api_login_returns_user_on_valid_credentials` -- POST `/api/auth/login` with valid username/password, assert 200 and `user` key in response.

2. `test_api_login_fails_with_wrong_password` -- POST `/api/auth/login` with invalid password, assert 401 and `error` key.

3. `test_api_login_creates_audit_log` -- After successful login, assert `audit_logs` table contains a `login` action.

4. `test_api_login_failed_creates_audit_log` -- After failed login, assert `audit_logs` contains `login_failed`.

5. `test_api_me_returns_authenticated_user` -- GET `/api/auth/me` as authenticated user, assert 200 with user data.

6. `test_api_me_returns_401_for_guest` -- GET `/api/auth/me` without auth, assert 401/redirect.

7. `test_api_logout_invalidates_session` -- POST `/api/auth/logout`, assert 200, then GET `/api/auth/me` fails.

8. `test_api_step_up_verify_with_correct_password` -- POST `/api/auth/step-up` with correct password, assert 200 and `elevated_until` in response.

9. `test_api_step_up_verify_rejects_wrong_password` -- POST `/api/auth/step-up` with wrong password, assert 403.

### File: `tests/Feature/Api/ApiCatalogTest.php` (new)

Test cases:

1. `test_api_catalog_index_returns_paginated_services` -- Seed services, GET `/api/catalog`, assert 200 and JSON structure with `data` array.

2. `test_api_catalog_index_requires_auth` -- GET `/api/catalog` without auth, assert 401.

3. `test_api_catalog_search_filters_by_title` -- GET `/api/catalog?search=Statistics`, assert only matching services returned.

4. `test_api_catalog_filters_by_audience` -- GET `/api/catalog?audience=faculty`, assert filtering works.

5. `test_api_catalog_show_returns_service_detail` -- GET `/api/catalog/{id}`, assert 200 with service data.

6. `test_api_catalog_toggle_favorite` -- POST `/api/catalog/{id}/favorite`, assert 200 with `favorited: true`, then again assert `favorited: false`.

7. `test_api_catalog_favorites_returns_user_favorites` -- GET `/api/catalog/favorites`, assert only favorited services.

8. `test_api_catalog_dictionaries_returns_types` -- GET `/api/catalog/dictionaries`, assert response has `service_types` and `audiences`.

9. `test_api_catalog_store_requires_editor_role` -- POST `/api/catalog` as learner, assert 403.

10. `test_api_catalog_store_succeeds_for_editor` -- POST `/api/catalog` as editor with valid data, assert 201/200.

11. `test_api_catalog_update_succeeds_for_editor` -- PUT `/api/catalog/{id}` as editor, assert updated data.

12. `test_api_catalog_update_forbidden_for_learner` -- PUT `/api/catalog/{id}` as learner, assert 403.

### File: `tests/Feature/Api/ApiReservationTest.php` (new)

Test cases:

1. `test_api_create_reservation` -- POST `/api/reservations` with `time_slot_id`, assert 200/201 with reservation data in `Pending` status.

2. `test_api_create_reservation_requires_auth` -- POST `/api/reservations` without auth, assert 401.

3. `test_api_confirm_reservation` -- POST `/api/reservations/{id}/confirm`, assert `Confirmed` status.

4. `test_api_confirm_other_users_reservation_returns_403` -- Create reservation for user A, confirm as user B, assert 403.

5. `test_api_cancel_reservation` -- POST `/api/reservations/{id}/cancel`, assert `Cancelled` status and slot released.

6. `test_api_check_in_reservation` -- POST `/api/reservations/{id}/check-in` within window, assert `CheckedIn`.

7. `test_api_check_out_reservation` -- POST `/api/reservations/{id}/check-out` after check-in, assert `Completed`.

8. `test_api_reschedule_reservation` -- POST `/api/reservations/{id}/reschedule` with `new_time_slot_id`, assert new reservation returned.

9. `test_api_show_reservation_returns_own` -- GET `/api/reservations/{id}` as owner, assert 200.

10. `test_api_show_reservation_returns_403_for_other_user` -- GET `/api/reservations/{id}` as non-owner non-admin, assert 403.

11. `test_api_index_returns_only_own_reservations` -- Create reservations for two users, assert each only sees their own via GET `/api/reservations`.

12. `test_api_reservation_lifecycle_produces_audit_logs` -- Full create-confirm-checkin-checkout flow, assert all expected `AuditAction` values present in `audit_logs`.

### File: `tests/Feature/Api/ApiAdminTest.php` (new)

Test cases:

1. `test_api_admin_users_requires_admin_role` -- GET `/api/admin/users` as editor, assert 403.

2. `test_api_admin_users_requires_step_up` -- GET `/api/admin/users` as admin without step-up, assert 403 with `step_up_required` error (the middleware returns JSON 403 when `expectsJson()`).

3. `test_api_admin_users_accessible_with_step_up` -- Elevate session, GET `/api/admin/users`, assert 200.

4. `test_api_admin_change_role` -- PUT `/api/admin/users/{id}/role` with valid role, assert role changed.

5. `test_api_admin_deactivate_user` -- DELETE `/api/admin/users/{id}`, assert user deactivated.

6. `test_api_admin_cannot_deactivate_self` -- DELETE `/api/admin/users/{self}`, assert 422.

7. `test_api_admin_dictionary_crud` -- POST, PUT, DELETE on `/api/admin/dictionaries`, assert full lifecycle.

8. `test_api_admin_form_rule_crud` -- POST, PUT, DELETE on `/api/admin/form-rules`, assert full lifecycle.

9. `test_api_admin_learner_cannot_access` -- GET various admin routes as learner, assert 403.

For step-up in tests, set session directly: `session(['step_up_verified_at' => now()])` before making the request, matching the pattern from `StepUpAuthIntegrationTest`.

### File: `tests/Feature/Api/ApiTenantIsolationTest.php` (new)

Test cases:

1. `test_api_reservation_index_only_shows_own` -- Two learners, each with reservations. GET `/api/reservations` as each, assert isolation.

2. `test_api_reservation_show_returns_403_for_other_user` -- Learner A tries to GET `/api/reservations/{B's id}`, assert 403.

3. `test_api_reservation_cancel_returns_403_for_other_user` -- Learner A tries to cancel B's reservation via API.

4. `test_api_favorites_are_isolated` -- Two learners with different favorites, assert API returns only own.

---

## Issue 3: Step-Up Auth Test Contradiction

### Analysis

In `tests/Feature/Integration/StepUpAuthIntegrationTest.php`, line 122-128:

```php
public function test_import_does_not_require_step_up(): void
{
    $this->actingAs($this->admin);
    $response = $this->get('/admin/import');
    $response->assertStatus(200);
}
```

Looking at `routes/web.php` line 65:
```php
Route::middleware(['role:admin', 'step-up'])->prefix('admin')->group(function () {
    Route::get('/import', ImportManager::class)->name('admin.import');
    Route::get('/export', ExportManager::class)->name('admin.export');
    ...
});
```

`/admin/import` IS behind the `step-up` middleware. This test is incorrect -- it asserts 200 but should expect a redirect to `route('auth.step-up')`. This test may currently pass if a previous test in the class set `step_up_verified_at` in the session and session state leaked (though with `RefreshDatabase` and separate test instances this is unlikely). More likely it is a failing or skipped test.

### Fix

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/tests/Feature/Integration/StepUpAuthIntegrationTest.php`**

Change `test_import_does_not_require_step_up` to reflect reality. Two options:

**Option A** (if the intent was to confirm import DOES require step-up, matching export):
```php
public function test_import_requires_step_up(): void
{
    $this->actingAs($this->admin);
    $response = $this->get('/admin/import');
    $response->assertRedirect(route('auth.step-up'));
}
```

**Option B** (if the design intent was that import should NOT require step-up):
Move the import route out of the `step-up` middleware group in `routes/web.php`. This seems unlikely given the README states all admin routes require step-up.

Recommendation: Option A -- fix the test assertion. The route definition is correct; the test comment and assertion are wrong.

---

## Issue 4: README/Docker-Compose Inconsistency and .env in Delivery

### Analysis

**README says "5 containers"** (line 48: `# Verify all 5 containers are healthy`) but `docker-compose.yml` defines **6 services**: app, nginx, db, redis, queue, scheduler.

**`.env` with real secrets**: The `.env` file contains `APP_KEY=base64:rRlGxxED9jGe1SL8tqSiF88I3Wwy5R7JWtiJB/i/tMY=` and `DB_PASSWORD=researchhub_secret`. The `.gitignore` correctly excludes `.env`, so this is not tracked in git. However, the `.env.example` is the stock Laravel template (SQLite, `APP_KEY=` blank, `SESSION_LIFETIME=120`) and does not reflect this project's actual configuration.

### File Changes

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/README.md`**

Line 48: Change `# Verify all 5 containers are healthy` to `# Verify all 6 containers are healthy`.

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/.env.example`**

Update to be a proper project-specific template. Key changes from the stock Laravel defaults:
- `APP_NAME=ResearchHub`
- `APP_URL=http://localhost:4000`
- `DB_CONNECTION=pgsql` with `DB_HOST=db`, `DB_PORT=5432`, `DB_DATABASE=researchhub`, `DB_USERNAME=researchhub`, `DB_PASSWORD=` (blank, user fills in)
- `SESSION_DRIVER=database`, `SESSION_LIFETIME=20`, `SESSION_ENCRYPT=true`
- `QUEUE_CONNECTION=redis`
- `CACHE_STORE=redis`, `CACHE_PREFIX=researchhub_`
- `REDIS_HOST=redis`
- Remove `MEMCACHED_HOST`
- Add comment: `# Generate key with: php artisan key:generate`

**File: `/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t71/repo/.env`**

This file is not tracked by git (confirmed via `git ls-files`). If it was accidentally included in a delivery archive, ensure delivery scripts use `.env.example` instead. No code change needed -- this is a process/packaging concern.

---

## Implementation Sequence

**Phase 1 -- Audit logging fixes (low risk, self-contained)**
1. Add three new enum cases to `AuditAction.php`
2. Add audit calls to five methods in `ReservationService.php`
3. Run existing `ReservationTest` to verify nothing breaks

2. **`expireIfPending()` runs inside a queue job**: The `AuditService` captures `request()->ip()` and `request()->userAgent()`. In a queue context, there is no HTTP request. {"file_path": "/Users/hana/.claude/plans/calm-chasing-catmull.md", "content": "# Implementation Plan: Fix Audit Fail & Partial Pass Issues

## Context
A delivery audit found 8 Fail and 11 Partial Pass issues in this Laravel 11 + Livewire 3 research services reservation platform. This plan addresses all actionable issues grouped into 8 workstreams, ordered by dependency and severity.

---

## Workstream 1: API Auth Security Parity (Blocker + High)
**Issues:** #2 (API auth bypasses controls), #12/#16 (security divergence)

### Changes:
1. **`app/Actions/Auth/AttemptLogin.php`** — Add `bool $establishSession = true` param. When false, skip `Auth::login()` and `session()->regenerate()`. Pass `$user` back in `LoginResult`.
2. **`app/Actions/Auth/LoginResult.php`** (or equivalent) — Add `?User $user` property so API controller can create tokens.
3. **`app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php`** — Rewrite `login()` to use `AttemptLogin` action with `establishSession: false`. Map each `LoginResult` status to proper HTTP codes (401, 403, 422, 423). Rewrite `logout()` to revoke Sanctum token.
4. **`routes/api.php`** — Change auth middleware to `auth:sanctum`. Add `password.not-expired` middleware to protected group.
5. **`app/Http/Middleware/CheckRole.php`** — Add `expectsJson()` checks to return JSON 401/403 instead of redirects for API requests.

---

## Workstream 2: Tailwind CDN → Local Assets (High)
**Issues:** #3/#15 (offline-first broken by CDN)

### Changes:
1. **`resources/views/components/layouts/app.blade.php`** line 8 — Replace `<script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>` with `@vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])`.
2. **`resources/views/components/layouts/guest.blade.php`** line 8 — Same replacement.
3. Run `npm install && npm run build` to compile local Tailwind.

**Note:** Project already has `tailwind.config.js`, `postcss.config.js`, `vite.config.js`, and `resources/css/app.css` with `@tailwind` directives. Only the layout references need updating.

---

## Workstream 3: Import Pipeline File Selection Bug (High)
**Issues:** #4 (cross-batch data integrity risk)

### Changes:
1. **New migration** `database/migrations/2026_04_09_000001_add_stored_path_to_import_batches.php` — Add `stored_path` varchar(500) nullable column.
2. **`app/Models/ImportBatch.php`** — Add `'stored_path'` to `$fillable`.
3. **`app/Livewire/Admin/ImportManager.php`**:
   - In `upload()`: add `'stored_path' => $path` to `ImportBatch::create()`.
   - In `startProcessing()`: replace glob-based file lookup (lines 113-125) with `storage_path('app/' . $batch->stored_path)`.

---

## Workstream 4: Dynamic Form Rules Enforcement (High)
**Issues:** #5/#13 (form rules configured but never enforced)

### Changes:
1. **New file** `app/Actions/Concerns/MergesDynamicRules.php` — Trait with `mergeRules(array $baseRules, string $entity, DataDictionaryService $dictService): array` that merges `getValidationRules()` output into base rules.
2. **`app/Actions/Catalog/ManageService.php`** — Use trait. Add `validationRules(string $mode = 'create'): array` method returning base rules merged with dynamic rules. Call validate in `create()`/`update()`.
3. **`app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`** — Replace hardcoded validation in `save()` with `$action->validationRules()`.
4. **`app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`** — Replace hardcoded validation in `store()`/`update()` with `$action->validationRules()`.
5. **`app/Http/Controllers/Api/ReservationController.php`** — Merge dynamic rules for `'reservation'` entity in `store()`.

---

## Workstream 5: Reservation Audit Logging Completeness (High)
**Issues:** #6/#14 (policy executions not fully logged)

### Changes:
1. **`app/Enums/AuditAction.php`** — Add three new cases:
   - `ReservationExpired = 'reservation_expired'`
   - `ReservationNoShow = 'reservation_no_show'`
   - `ReservationPartialAttendance = 'reservation_partial_attendance'`

2. **`app/Services/ReservationService.php`** — Add audit calls in 5 methods:
   - `reschedule()` (~line 162): Log `ReservationRescheduled` with old/new slot IDs.
   - `checkIn()` (~line 184): Use `ReservationPartialAttendance` when late, `ReservationCheckedIn` when on-time.
   - `checkOut()` (~line 211): Log `ReservationCheckedOut`.
   - `expireIfPending()` (~line 229): Log `ReservationExpired` with `triggered_by => 'system'`.
   - `processNoShows()` (~line 256): Log `ReservationNoShow` per reservation with `'warning'` severity.

---

## Workstream 6: Project/Patent Duplicate Keys (High)
**Issues:** #7 (missing duplicate criteria)

### Changes:
1. **New migration** `database/migrations/2026_04_09_000002_add_project_patent_to_services.php` — Add `project_number` (unique, nullable) and `patent_number` (unique, nullable) to services.
2. **`app/Models/Service.php`** — Add to `$fillable`.
3. **`app/Services/ImportExportService.php`**:
   - `detectServiceDuplicates()`: Add exact-match checks for project_number/patent_number before trigram.
   - `exportServices()`: Include new fields.
   - `getDestinationFields()`: Add fields to mapping list.
   - `createServiceFromImport()`: Include new fields.
4. **`app/Http/Controllers/Api/CatalogController.php`** — Add validation rules for new fields.
5. **`app/Livewire/Catalog/ServiceManager.php`** — Add properties and form fields.
6. **`app/Http/Resources/ServiceResource.php`** — Include new fields.
7. **`resources/views/livewire/catalog/service-manager.blade.php`** — Add input fields.

---

## Workstream 7: API Test Coverage (High)
**Issues:** #8/#17/#18/#19 (API paths untested)

### New test files:
1. **`tests/Feature/Api/ApiAuthTest.php`** — Login lockout, CAPTCHA requirement, password expiry, token management, logout (~10 tests).
2. **`tests/Feature/Api/ApiCatalogTest.php`** — Filtering, sorting, pagination, favorites, RBAC 403 for writes (~12 tests).
3. **`tests/Feature/Api/ApiReservationTest.php`** — Full lifecycle, 401/403/404, object-level isolation, audit assertions (~12 tests).
4. **`tests/Feature/Api/ApiAdminTest.php`** — Step-up enforcement, RBAC, dictionary/form-rule CRUD (~9 tests).
5. **`tests/Feature/Api/ApiTenantIsolationTest.php`** — Cross-user reservation/favorite isolation (~4 tests).

Use `$this->getJson()`/`$this->postJson()` for JSON responses. Use `$this->withSession(['step_up_verified_at' => now()])` for step-up tests.

---

## Workstream 8: Documentation & Config Cleanup (Medium)
**Issues:** #9 (README inconsistency), #10 (step-up test contradiction), #11 (.env in delivery)

### Changes:
1. **`README.md`** line 48 — Change "5 containers" to "6 containers".
2. **`tests/Feature/Integration/StepUpAuthIntegrationTest.php`** lines 122-128 — Fix `test_import_does_not_require_step_up` to assert redirect (import IS behind step-up middleware).
3. **`.env.example`** — Update to match project config: pgsql, researchhub DB, session lifetime 20, redis cache/queue, blank APP_KEY with generation comment.
4. **`docs/architecture-decision-api-livewire.md`** — Add "Shared Service Contract" section documenting that validation, audit, and auth are enforced at the Action/Service layer shared by both paths.

---

## Execution Order

| Phase | Workstream | Risk | Dependencies |
|-------|-----------|------|-------------|
| 1 | WS2: Tailwind CDN | Low | None |
| 2 | WS3: Import file path | Low | None |
| 3 | WS5: Audit logging | Low | None |
| 4 | WS8: Docs/config cleanup | Low | None |
| 5 | WS6: Project/patent fields | Medium | None |
| 6 | WS4: Dynamic form rules | Medium | None |
| 7 | WS1: API auth parity | Medium | WS4 (shared validation) |
| 8 | WS7: API tests | Medium | WS1, WS5, WS6 (tests verify fixes) |

Phases 1-4 are independent and can run in parallel. Phase 5-6 are independent. Phase 7 depends on 6. Phase 8 depends on 5, 6, 7.

## Verification
1. `npm run build` — Confirms Tailwind compiles locally
2. `php artisan migrate` — Confirms new migrations apply
3. `php artisan test` — Full test suite passes including new API tests
4. Manual check: grep for `cdn.tailwindcss.com` returns zero matches
5. Manual check: grep for `glob(` in ImportManager returns zero matches
"}
